description_long: This object is a highly detailed, stylized pink lotus flower with multiple layers of broad, pointed petals radiating outward from a central core. The petals are arranged in a whorled pattern, overlapping each other and creating a sense of depth and fullness. Each petal displays a gradient of pink hues, with darker pinks at the base and lighter, almost white, tips, and subtle striping along their length. The flower lacks a visible stem or leaves, giving it a floating, sculptural appearance. The surface of the petals appears smooth and slightly glossy, mimicking the delicate texture of real lotus petals. This flower would be visually striking as a decorative element in urban parks, water features, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A stylized pink lotus flower with layered, pointed petals and a smooth, glossy surface. The petals display a gradient of pink shades and are arranged in a whorled, overlapping pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full, symmetrical arrangement of overlapping pink petals radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and layering of the petals, with more visible petal overlap.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the radial symmetry and the pointed tips of the petals.
description_view_3: Similar to the previous view, showing the whorled petal arrangement and subtle color gradients.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled, radial configuration, with multiple layers of overlapping petals radiating symmetrically from the center. Each layer is offset from the previous, creating a dense, full appearance typical of lotus flowers.
